The main claim is: An annual benchmarking event has advantages over static, run-any-time benchmarks when it comes to testing generality.

Once a year, we’d run a benchmark or suite of benchmarks against the most generally intelligent AI systems available to us at the time, seeking to assess their generality and the overall shape of their capabilities.
